programme.title: Outside: Beyond the Lens
programme.desc: An age-old practice of knitting lace by Benedictine nuns; cooking a sacred dish by locals on Pakleni Islands.

programme.title: Finding Your Roots With Henry Louis Gates, Jr.
programme.desc: Actor Michael Douglas and writer/director Lena Dunham explore their family roots.

programme.title: The American Revolution
programme.desc: British General Cornwallis struggles to pacify the southern states; supported by the French, a victory at Yorktown secures independence for the Americans.

programme.title: Miles, Morale and Memories: Bob Hope and World War II
programme.desc: Animated maps and interviews with Hollywood historians, authors and combat veterans illustrate Bob Hope's impact on World War II.

programme.title: Amanpour and Company
programme.desc: Christiane Amanpour leads discussions about world issues and interviews with global leaders.

programme.title: This Land Is Your Land (My Music Presents)
programme.desc: The Smothers Brothers and Judy Collins host a look at the evolution of modern American folk music; performers include Glenn Yarborough, the Highwaymen, the Kingston Trio and the Limeliters.

programme.title: 50 Years With Peter, Paul and Mary
programme.desc: Performance footage of folk group Peter, Paul and Mary.

programme.title: Lyla in the Loop
programme.desc: Lyla trains Stu to help at the diner; Stu learns how to ride a scooter.

programme.title: Wild Kratts
programme.desc: The Wild Kratts set off to find and re-create spider silk.

programme.title: Arthur
programme.sub-title: Arthur Plays the Blues; Buster's Sweet Success
programme.desc: Arthur gets a new piano teacher; Buster volunteers to sell candy for the band.

programme.title: Weather Hunters
programme.desc: Benny's toy penguin is swept away by melting snow, transforming a day of sledding into an urgent rescue mission.

programme.desc: Lyla trains Stu to help at the diner; Stu learns how to ride a scooter.

programme.title: Carl the Collector
programme.sub-title: A Forrest of Plans; The Marble Collection
programme.desc: Forrest makes too many plans on the same day; Carl and Arugula build an epic marble run but fail to get the marble to run.

programme.title: Daniel Tiger's Neighborhood
programme.sub-title: The Family Campout; A Game Night for Everyone
programme.desc: Daniel and Dad Tiger go to the Dad and Me Camp-out, where they run into Katerina, who arrives with her mom; Daniel and Mom head to the Mom and Me Game Night.

programme.title: Phoebe & Jay
programme.desc: Phoebe and Jay learn how to sort items; Phoebe and Jay learn how to make food labels, so everyone can participate in the potluck.

programme.title: Sesame Street
programme.desc: When Telly learns that earthworms are invited to Abby's garden party, his friends help him feel less scared.

programme.title: Work It Out Wombats!
programme.desc: Sammy plans a surprise Carnaval for his dad while Quique plans a surprise treat for his son; Malik performs a magic trick.

programme.title: Donkey Hodie
programme.sub-title: Bob Dog's Stuffie Situation; A Swampy Playdate
programme.desc: Bob Dog brings a ball to the Stuffie Playdate; Frannie Fox invites Donkey to her swamp, but she feels nervous about playing in it.

programme.title: Rosie's Rules
programme.sub-title: Donating Day; Gatita the Volunteer Cat
programme.desc: Rosie accidentally gives away her doll Lele on Donating Day; Rosie and Galita volunteer for Seniors Day at the Nature Center.

programme.title: Elinor Wonders Why
programme.sub-title: Melting Point; Mr. Raccoon's Bad Day
programme.desc: While delivering chocolates, the kids learn that warmer temperatures can melt treats; the kids help Mr. Raccoon calm down when he is having a bad day.

programme.title: Alma's Way
programme.sub-title: Alma Goes to Puerto Rico: Bisabuela's Birthday
programme.desc: On Alma's great-grandmother's 100th birthday, Alma wants to find Bisabuela the perfect gift; even though she can think of all kinds of things that Bisabuela likes, none of them are quite right.

programme.title: Molly of Denali
programme.sub-title: Lynx to the Past; Molly of the Yukon
programme.desc: Trini is discouraged because she can't add her own moves to the Lynx Dance; Molly hopes to catch a Yukon River king salmon.

programme.title: Finding Your Roots With Henry Louis Gates, Jr.
programme.desc: Actor Michael Douglas and writer/director Lena Dunham explore their family roots.

programme.title: American Muslims: A History Revealed
programme.desc: Even before the United States is founded, tens of thousands of Muslims are already here, captured in West Africa and brought to colonial America in chains; Asma Khalid tells the story of one of these people.

programme.title: American Muslims: A History Revealed
programme.desc: Thomas Jefferson owned a copy of the Qur'an; George Washington owned enslaved people who were Muslim; a Muslim diplomat breaks his Ramadan fast in the White House in 1805.

programme.title: Journey Home to the USS Arizona
programme.desc: The family of Raymond Haerry Sr. travels from New Jersey to Oahu, Hawaii, to place his ashes at the USS Arizona Memorial at Pearl Harbor.

programme.title: BBC News: The Context USA
programme.desc: Coverage from Washington, D.C. and London, with a regular panel of guests from across the political spectrum discussing the biggest stories of the day.

programme.title: BBC News America
programme.desc: Live news program produced out of BBC's Washington, D.C. bureau; host Sumi Somaskanda.

programme.title: PBS News Hour
programme.desc: Co-anchors Amna Nawaz and Geoff Bennett and correspondents offer in-depth analysis of current events.

programme.title: Energy Switch
programme.desc: Native Americans build energy projects on tribal lands to change their future; with CEO Cheri Smith, Alliance for Tribal Clean Energy and COO Ken Ahmann, Colusa Indian Energy.

programme.title: American Grown: My Job Depends on Ag
programme.desc: Two men discuss farming, California water policies and the future of agriculture.

programme.title: Life on Earth: Attenborough's Greatest Adventure
programme.desc: The story behind David Attenborough's wildlife documentary series "Life on Earth," which changed his life forever.

programme.title: NOVA
programme.desc: How discoveries over the past 50 years have revolutionized people's understanding of the universe.

programme.title: Shared Planet
programme.desc: From Kenya to Mexico, people and wildlife find a harmonious balance in Earth's most vulnerable landscapes.

programme.title: Life on Earth: Attenborough's Greatest Adventure
programme.desc: The story behind David Attenborough's wildlife documentary series "Life on Earth," which changed his life forever.

programme.title: Amanpour and Company
programme.desc: Christiane Amanpour leads discussions about world issues and interviews with global leaders.

programme.title: The Mustangs: America's Wild Horses
programme.desc: With more than 80,000 wild horses on federal lands and more than 50,000 in government corrals, filmmakers Steven Latham and Conrad Stanley reveal why the protection of America's wild horses is more important than ever.

programme.title: Great Scenic Railway Journeys 30th Anniversary Special
programme.desc: Some of America's best railway adventures, from the Rocky Mountaineer to the Skyrail, these stories offer a glimpse into our nation's railroad history and explore how railroads defined the spirit of North America.

programme.desc: Lyla trains Stu to help at the diner; Stu learns how to ride a scooter.

programme.desc: Martin encounters a lost colony of black-footed ferrets.
